data: Special bottle (BACTEC Myco/F Lytic culture bottle) should be used. DO NOT REFRIGERATE bone marrow and blood AFB culture specimens, store them at 20-25 degree C.

Collect pus, purulent exudate, aspirate, or wound biopsy specimens in a sterile container without fixative. DO NOT SEND SWABS unless there is no other means. Dry swabs are unacceptable. If the specimen must be stored, refrigerate it immediately after specimen collection and after office hours.

abscess aspirate, abscess swab, ascitic fluid, bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL), urine (bladder, ureteric, nephrostomy, suprapubic), bone, bronchial aspirate, curettage,drainage, endotracheal aspirate, fluid, gastric lavage, hydrocele fluid, joint fluid, peritoneal dialysis fluid(PDF), pericardial fluid, peritoneal fluid (non-PDF), pleural fluid, pus aspirate, sputum, tissue/biopsy, tracheostomy aspirate

abscess aspirate, abscess swab, ascitic fluid, bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL), urine (bladder, ureteric, nephrostomy, suprapubic), bone, bronchial aspirate, curettage,drainage, endotracheal aspirate, fluid, gastric lavage, hydrocele fluid, joint fluid, peritoneal dialysis fluid(PDF), pericardial fluid, peritoneal fluid (non-PDF), pleural fluid, pus aspirate, sputum, tissue/biopsy, tracheostomy aspirate
Tuberculosis (TB), infections by Mycobacterium avium-intracellulare complex (MAI), nontuberculous mycobacterium (NTM), mycobacteria other than tuberculosis (MOTT)
Acknowledge and store at 4°C, except blood culture or BM (using special bottle), which is placed at room temperature.
